context: Tests whether the agent implements webhook subscription management with a cryptographically secure signing secret that is shown only once, URL reachability validation with correct error handling, and DLQ monitoring that alerts when the queue depth exceeds 10 events.

checklist.name: 32-byte random secret
checklist.description: The signing secret is generated using crypto.randomBytes(32).toString('hex'), producing a 64-character hex string

checklist.name: Secret shown once only
checklist.description: The API response includes the signingSecret value, AND the code/README explicitly states that the secret will not be shown again after creation (not stored retrievably or re-exposed in subsequent API calls)

checklist.name: URL reachability check
checklist.description: Before creating a subscription, the code attempts a POST request to the provided URL to verify it is reachable

checklist.name: URL validation timeout
checklist.description: The URL validation fetch uses AbortSignal.timeout(5000) or equivalent 5-second timeout

checklist.name: Any status acceptable for validation
checklist.description: The URL validation accepts any HTTP response status as valid (does NOT reject based on non-2xx status), only failing if the request itself throws/times out

checklist.name: 400 returned on unreachable URL
checklist.description: Returns HTTP 400 (not 500 or other status) when the URL validation request fails/times out

checklist.name: DLQ threshold alert at 10
checklist.description: The monitoring logic triggers an alert when the dead-letter queue count for a subscriber meets or exceeds 10 events (not a higher or unspecified threshold)

checklist.name: DLQ alert mechanism
checklist.description: The monitoring function calls an alerting function (Slack, PagerDuty, or generic notification) when the threshold is exceeded, not just logs to console

checklist.name: node:crypto used for secret
checklist.description: Uses the built-in node:crypto module (randomBytes) rather than a third-party library or Math.random() for secret generation

checklist.name: Subscription stores events list
checklist.description: The subscription record stores a list of event types the subscriber is interested in (e.g., events: string[])

checklist.name: isActive flag on subscription
checklist.description: The subscription record includes an isActive (or equivalent enabled/active) boolean field
